title: Chat templates
description: Formatting rules that turn role-tagged messages into the exact token sequence a chat-tuned model expects.
openingSummary: Chat templates wrap system, user, and assistant turns with model-specific markers so instruction-tuned decoders see the same token pattern at inference as during alignment.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A chat template is a small program—often Jinja embedded in tokenizer config—that serializes message lists into one prompt string before tokenization. It chooses role headers, turn separators, and whether tool outputs are inlined. Hugging Face tokenizer.chat_template fields and llama.cpp prompt builders are common carriers.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: The same weights behave differently when templates omit system prompts or swap user/assistant markers. API gateways that concatenate raw strings without the template see degraded instruction following even with identical logits kernels. Templates also determine where generation should start and stop.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: Two user turns might render as <|system|>...<|user|>question<|assistant|> before the model continues autoregressively from the assistant slot.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: Chat templates are not the tokenizer algorithm—they sit after message structuring and before encode(). They are also not the same as system prompts stored in product UI; the template defines syntax, not business content.
